text1:
The Gale Cannon sacrifices range and rate of fire for the ability to punch through most armor with a single well-timed hit.

Firing heavy shaped-charge rounds, it can blow a hole in most military-grade armor with one shot. As they were designed to be reliable, the barrel and turret house can withstand vacuum welding and spalling for a long time, making it a low-maintenance weapon suitable for long-range missions. Oversized ammo and a focus on service life means that the autoloader and heatsinks have been downprioritized, giving the Gale Cannon a very low rate of fire.

Most of the turret house consists of recoil-absorbing servos and gel reservoirs, making it feasible to mount the cannon on smaller ships without exceeding ordnance budgets. Because its effective range is low, it serves best as a main gun aboard agile frigates and assault destroyers.
An accurate quad-barrel autocannon that will quickly hammer its way through shields (and lightly-armored obstacles) at close range.

A midrange weapon useful against all targets, that sacrifices some efficiency for more power vs. armor plating than standard kinetic cannons.

The Shard Cannon draws its name from its special ammunition, the Phase Shard. Made in a reactor that constantly displaces a growing crystal between phase-space and normal-space, they carry an interdimensional vibration within their molecular bonds. The Shard Cannon fire control assembly induces an oscillation right before a round leaves the barrel, making it volatile. If an activated Shard shatters and lodges itself inside a foreign material, the disrupted standing wave can cause twisted particles to return to the universe as antimatter and subsequently annihilate. A major drawback is that no Shard will shatter in the same way, so only a small portion of hits generate a reaction. Luckily, the overall frequency of detonations is high enough that it grants the weapon added power against armor and hull.
Fires massive bolts of antimatter with devastating results. Has a firing delay that must be compensated for. Holds charges that replenish over time. Payload size can vary slightly.

An advanced missile that splits into three heatseekers with EMP warheads. Its superhot payload deals extra damage to hull and armor, and can obliterate unarmored targets.

Time and time again, detractors have pointed out that the weapon keeps ending up in the wrong hands, where its utter lethality against poorly protected ships takes a toll on Sector shipping. Blackrock Drive Yards representatives maintain that with proper measures (and Blackrock technology, especially) the playing field can be evened out, and that the Salamander MRM is still more popular outside of Blackrock fleets.

A pod of advanced missiles that split into three heatseekers with EMP warheads. Their superhot payload deals extra damage to hull and armor, and can obliterate unarmored targets.

Time and time again, detractors have pointed out that the weapon keeps ending up in the wrong hands, where its utter lethality against poorly protected ships takes a toll on Sector shipping. Blackrock Drive Yards representatives maintain that with proper measures (and Blackrock technology, especially) the playing field can be evened out, and that the Salamander MRM is still more popular outside of Corporate fleets.

Launches Shard-tipped rockets that accelerate to great speeds. Chance for a secondary effect if the rockets strike armor.

The Quill is a simple anti-shield weapon that uses a rocket to deliver a massive Shard warhead. Against light armor, repeated hits from Quill rockets can be a serious threat, as the detonations are heavier than those of cannon-fired Shard rounds.

Launches a barrage of Shard-tipped rockets that accelerate to great speeds. Chance for a secondary effect if the rockets strike armor.

The Quill is a simple anti-shield weapon that uses a rocket to deliver a massive Shard warhead. Against light armor, repeated hits from Quill rockets can be a serious threat, as the detonations are heavier than those of cannon-fired Shard rounds.

Fires a jet of magnetic fluid that focuses a great deal of kinetic energy on a single point and moves at incredible speeds. Great against weak armor, small ships that are normally hard to hit, or for inflicting bursts of damage when several Ferroguns are fired together.

A complicated set of pressure vessels, magnetic coils and nearly frictionless coatings are required for the Ferrogun to operate smoothly. Once fired, the jet of ferrofluid maintains integrity thanks to the properties of the particles embedded in the fluid, and a combination of sheer kinetic energy and static charge makes it very effective against shields. The Ferrogun also easily punctures light spaced armor, which is devastating against smaller targets with little space between their armor belts and vital hull components.

Blackrock also manufactures an anti-personnel version of the Ferrogun for atmospheric and planetside combat. With low effective range compared to vacuum operation, the Type-II Ferrogun compensates by firing a flammable compound that becomes sticky when exposed to atmospheric gases, punching power-armored troops to the ground and rendering them incapable of movement

Features a rotating drum that fires into a single barrel, yielding a powerful opening barrage, while sustained output is lower due to heatsink performance and ammo loading.

Thanks to its design, maintenance for the INM is practical, as ammo drums/firing assemblies can be replaced wholesale.
Due to the Corporation's long history, the acronym this weapon is known for has lost its original meaning, but it may refer to the ''Ichneumon''-type anti-armor shell commonly used with the gun. The shells are designed to penetrate as deep into spaced armor as possible before detonating. This ensures a greater overall destructive effect per round than with shells of a similar caliber.

Quickly neutralizes any unshielded fighter. Projects a very wide beam that pierces and destroys missiles, but prioritizes targeting fighters before missiles. Can be an asset when knife-fighting enemy ships, as well.

Shoots a single bolt of exotic energy that deals enormous damage.

The Scalaron Blaster is outwardly similar to the Heavy Blaster: it fires an energy bolt that travels inside a magnetic containment field. The difference is that this weapon uses a compact dimensional vortex to alter its payload before firing. Plasma is screened through pocket dimensions, interacting differently with spacetime once it reenters the universe - it takes longer to dissipate, travels further and causes more destruction than purely baryonic plasma, due to a wide range of poorly understood effects.

Hulls blown apart by a Scalaron Blaster are pulverized down to the subatomic level: a fog of razor-sharp nanoparticles tends to form and then cling to surfaces, lethal to unprotected crew and equipment alike. This side effect is sometimes exploited to combat reverse engineering.

Useful for destroying hardened targets, the S-Blaster can be found on fast strike ships, even though the feedback generated by the weapon is hard to handle for most reactors.

A plasma torpedo with an extremely powerful engine, that can be launched with impunity to overload weak shields or breach armor. Builds flux when launched.

The T-1 Fury is Blackrock's most widely-exported missile weapon. Simple in construction, the torpedo's powerplant is also its payload. The initial burn takes the reactor into redline, which then generates a massive ball of plasma from the shock of impacting a shield or hull. It's especially good against medium-armored ships with weak shields, but its yield is drastically lower than antimatter or nuclear-based torpedoes.

A small-caliber dual rotary cannon that fires in short bursts and then reloads. Highly effective against missiles, fighters, and unarmored targets.

Regarded as the most important weapon among Blackrock's exports, the Mark II ''Shredder'' Dual Rotary Cannon is cheap, easy to mount on practically any ship, and grants a lot of utility. This is aided by Blackrock usually exporting and selling them in batches, as Corporation doctrine recommends setting up batteries of several Shredders acting together to provide sufficient protection against missiles at high traverse. Experienced captains prefer the Shredder mostly for its ability to chew through weakened ships, as the longer barrel and greater ammo caliber compared to the Vulcan enables the Shredder to successfully fire into enemy warships at medium range, where its shaped fragmentation rounds make quick work of exposed hull sections.

Fires a scattering blast of high-velocity flechettes, great against shields. Tends to easily cause overloads, but they are short due to low per-shot damage. Corporation doctrine puts emphasis on combining Volley Guns with anti-armor weapons for greater kill power.

The standard-issue Hyperkinetic Volley Gun loads canisters of metallic nanocolloid that are programmed to morph when subjected to thermic shock: when fired by a chemical propellant, this colloid turns into a chaos of monomolecular needles that fan out into a buckshot pattern, saturating a shield barrier with pin-prick impacts.

The drawback of this weapon is its ineffectiveness versus armor. If not combined with other weapons, the Volley Gun is relatively incapable of actually damaging ships on its own, but its raw power can threaten exposed hulls.

Fires a dense jet of ferrofluid that inflicts great damage and is hard to avoid even at max range thanks to its incredible speed and accuracy. Will usually punch clean through weakened armor, and direct hits will disrupt enemy systems thanks to the jet's electric charge.

The strength of the Ferrocannon lies with the low mass of its payload. While other accelerator weapons fire a massive slug, the Ferrocannon fires a focused burst of magnetic liquid with embedded nanoparticulate 'shrapnel'. This payload moves much faster than any conventional slug, and delivers a similar amount of kinetic energy. Its massive canisters of pressurized ammo and its power requirements makes the Ferrocannon hard to mount on most ships. The Karkinos can famously mount a battery of three Ferrocannons on its own, projecting a massive kill zone against enemy frigates and destroyers.

Shoots Gale rounds in a one-two-three pattern. A barrage from this weapon will devastate even the thickest armor, and puts strain on weak shield emitters.

Desiring a heavy-duty anti-capital weapon for their new line of capital and subcapital warships, Blackrock decided on a simple solution: Take an already mass-produced medium strike gun, and figure out a way to mount three of them together with one ammo drum, one targeting array and one turret house. While the Gale Battery needs to fire in sequence to handle the considerable recoil of its chemical propellant, each round alone packs enough firepower to cripple a frigate. The Gale Battery is a serious threat to slower, hardened targets, and maintains the economical ordnance profile of its smaller cousin. Its biggest drawback is the relatively low fire rate that comes as an unavoidable tradeoff from the caliber of the Gale round.

Developed from the Mark IV Self-Propelled Asteroid Impactor, this enormous missile is a useful shock weapon. While entirely unguided, a direct hit will usually lead to a long and punishing overload, and the kinetic energy delivered will push ships away from the impact.

A twin-linked version of the Shard Cannon, decently useful against all targets. Sacrifices some efficiency for more power vs. armor plating than standard kinetic cannons.

The Shard Cannon draws its name from its special ammunition, the Phase Shard. Made in a reactor that constantly displaces a growing crystal between phase-space and normal-space, they carry an interdimensional vibration within their molecular bonds. The Shard Cannon induces an oscillation right before a round leaves the barrel, making it volatile. If an activated Shard shatters and lodges itself inside a foreign material, the disrupted standing wave can cause twisted particles to return to the universe as antimatter and subsequently annihilate. A major drawback is that no Shard will shatter in the same way, so just a small portion of hits generate a reaction.

A rack of plasma torpedoes with extremely powerful engines, that can be launched with impunity to overload weak shields or breach armor. Builds flux when launched.

The T-1 Fury is Blackrock's most widely-exported missile weapon. Simple in construction, the torpedo's powerplant is also its payload. The initial burn takes the reactor into redline, which then generates a massive ball of plasma from the shock of impacting a shield or hull. It's especially good against medium-armored ships with weak shields, but its yield is drastically lower than antimatter-based torpedoes.
A well-rounded plasma discharge system that can burn its way through thick armor. Creates a window of opportunity to apply concentrated firepower to enemy shields.
The most compact plasma discharge weapon in Blackrock's arsenal, the Sunjet can rapidly melt through armor if given the chance. Needs help from other weapons to threaten shielded targets.

Two Shredders on a high-performance mount with improved ammo feeders and cooling, for a total of four barrels: a potent close-in weapon system. Obliterates unarmored targets.

The Mark I Quad Shredder Close-In Weapon System is another popular Blackrock export, valued for its ability to blanket an area of space with lethal frag rounds. Each gun has several small improvements compared to the Mark II Dual Shredder Rotary Cannon, which add up to an absolutely lethal amount of raw damage that no weakened target can stand up to for long. Since it has decent capability as an anti-fighter, anti-missile and anti-ship weapon, the Shredder Battery really is a worthwhile investment on any ship that can afford to mount it, which any Blackrock associate will enthusiastically tell you, should you approach them with arms-related inquiries.

An energy weapon that deals great damage at the cost of range and accuracy. Lethal at point-blank ranges and very flux-efficient.

The Linear Cycle Pulse Gun, or LPG for short, uses an oscillating magnetic field to spit out self-contained energy packets at a steady rate. The pulsing guidance field ensures very little energy is required to maintain a rapid rate of fire, but it also makes the weapon inaccurate. Compared to pulsed laser weapons, the LPG certainly punches far above its weight class, but its very low range makes it best suited for small, fast ships, or as a knife fighting weapon on a larger vessel.

An upgrade of the Gale Cannon with a built-in targeting unit and second-stage magnetic acceleration mechanism. Trades fire rate for power, range and muzzle velocity, making it excellent for opportunistic attacks against distracted ships or for barrage fire with heavy kinetic weapons.

Fires a short-ranged, pinpoint ray that atomizes incoming missiles and damages fighters, but does little against enemy ships.

The Argus Particle Beam is a deceptively powerful weapon. While not useful against the radiation-hardened armor of most warships, the ray fired by the Argus is devastating to unprotected targets like fighters or missiles. A single Argus can protect a ship against stray missiles and heatseekers, while several acting together can form a nearly impenetrable defense.

A short-range weapon useful against all targets, thanks to special ammunition that increases efficiency against armor compared to inert kinetic slugs.

This variant of the Shard Cannon is designed to unleash a constant stream of smaller shards rather than relying on burst damage. Three revolving internal ammo drums rotate in a triangular pattern, allowing individual drums to be rapidly cooled during the inactive part of the firing cycle. Its mass-produced Type III ammo is fairly simple and does not pack as much of a punch as the Type II used by the Shard Cannon.

Fires a volley of volatile shrapnel that obliterates any lightly-armored obstacle in its way. When fired into a hull breach, tends to make short work of even large ships. Autofire AI priority is fighters, then missiles, then enemy ships.

The Ironweaver Area Denial Cannon fires 'honeycombs' of ammunition feedmass designed to deform into a chaos of hot, rigid splinters when subjected to the weapon's complicated firing cycle. Each of these ammo combs is the size of a grown man, and requires a deep ammunition elevator to be built into the turret module of the mounting ship. An array of these weapons can turn space into a lethal hailstorm of shrapnel. Effective against light targets due to its saturation effect, the Ironweaver is also surprisingly useful against large shield barriers that tend to absorb entire volleys at once.
A miniaturized particle beam, with an optimized capacitor system that lets it fire without flux buildup. A decent option against stray missiles.
An unpredictable plasma missile, good in swarms and against frigates. Firing each missile builds flux.

Opens a dimensional rift that unleashes bolts of unstable energy. Devastating at close range, but unreliable at medium range. Rapidly regains charges after firing. Tapping shots is recommended at longer ranges.

Fires a lethal stream of exotic energy bolts. Needs to charge up briefly before firing, and inaccurate during long bursts, but has a staggering potential for raw damage output.

An offshoot of the Imaginos Rift Cannon technology, this advanced weapon uses the bare minimum of interdimensional connectivity to charge its plasma bolts, and is much weaker than the Rift Cannon, but far more reliable. Designed as a hybrid assault/suppression weapon, the Repeater works best when mounted on a ship that can avoid attracting enemy fire, powering a sustained barrage to lock a target into a defensive position. Alternatively, it can be mounted on ships with powerful reactors to directly convert their energy output into overwhelming firepower for a strong head-on assault.
A defensive weapon only present on Dimensional Engine ships. Reliable against missiles and fighters, but can also be used against enemy ships at close range.
A pod of unpredictable plasma missiles, excellent for providing cover during an advance or destroying enemy frigates. Firing each missile builds flux.

Launches a barrage of light missiles that are impossible to evade, effective against light targets, fighters and exposed hull. Comes with an autoloader.

An ever-popular Blackrock export, the Dart SRM fires an incredibly simple missile optimized for high-acceleration thrust vectoring and interception. Easily manufactured from fabrication feedmass, the optimized Dart missile features a very delicately balanced ratio of reaction mass to payload mass, enabling unmatched pursuit performance, while the launcher rapidly primes and launches large swarms designed to overwhelm even the most diehard fighter ace.

The unsophisticated directed frag warhead is easily defeated by any meaningful level of armor protection, but the Dart enjoys a reputation for wreaking havoc on damaged ships, which is why it tends to be used far more heavily than what its intended role would suggest.

Launches a withering barrage of light missiles that are impossible to evade, effective against light targets, fighters and exposed hull. Comes with an autoloader, expanded volley size and expanded ammunition compared to the Light Dart.

An ever-popular Blackrock export, the Dart SRM fires an incredibly simple missile optimized for high-acceleration thrust vectoring and interception. Easily manufactured from fabrication feedmass, the optimized Dart missile features a very delicately balanced ratio of reaction mass to warhead mass, enabling unmatched pursuit performance, while the launcher rapidly primes and launches large swarms designed to overwhelm even the most diehard fighter ace.

The unsophisticated directed frag warhead is easily defeated by any meaningful level of armor protection, but the Dart enjoys a reputation for wreaking havoc on damaged ships, which is why it tends to be used far more heavily than what its intended role would suggest.
This modified Gale Cannon is spinally mounted on the Stinger Drone, and can deliver respectable mid-range fire support.

This massive plasma projector can melt an exposed ship into unrecognizable slag. Forces enemies to rely on shields for defense, or face terrible consequences.

Sometimes referred to as the 'portable solar flare', the Sunstorm Plasma Discharge Emitter has some of the most advanced capacitor arrays and power conduits of any modular weapon in the Sector. Extremely precise control over magnetic fields and power surges are required for this weapon to not waste energy and misfire.

While shields are great at dissipating thermal energy, most types of armor cannot perform their intended function once reduced to a boiling, chaotic mass. This makes the Sunstorm a reliable anti-warship weapon for capitals.

A swift, powerful ship built around a core of potent technology, the Nevermore is known for its surprising speed and enormous destructive potential.

Originally designed to project capital-crushing firepower from a mobile subcapital platform, necessity forced the primary specification through several sidegrades before it could serve as a line cruiser. Since it can generate reaction mass for its main gun rather than storing it in bulky holds, the ship is fast for its size, and freedom of movement is its chief asset.

Most of the hull is occupied by critical systems, resulting in a cramped and utilitarian bridge and crew quarters. Sometimes, men will even bunk in the cargo holds to get a break from the crew compartments, which tend to heat up during hyperspace cruise. Still, low-ranking BRDY associates will practically climb over one another for a chance to serve aboard a Nevermore, knowing it's a start on the road to working with the Corporation's most exotic technology or serving in its most elite force projection fleets.

Controversial as a resource-hungry vessel of focused destruction, proponents of the Nevermore cite its importance as a deterrent, while critics view it as a symbol of the Corporation's arrogance and its intent to meddle with the Sector's power balance.

A relic from the Exodus, this destroyer is as fast as it is large and powerful. A logistical nightmare, vulnerable to strike weapons and EMP, the Desdinova has survived more than six attempts at retirement and replacement, due to its undeniable usefulness as a shock weapon and heavy interceptor.

Ostensibly created and then maintained by Blackrock to "deal with internal threats", the Desdinova is one of the most gratuitous warships ever designed. It's a well-known piece of Corporate trivia that this destroyer packs enough thrust to de-orbit large space platforms on its own; a feat which has actually been performed in the past. While decades of modernization have made up for some of the hull's flaws, the original Desdinova was not designed to survive more than 4-5 sorties before being broken down for its core components, and its drive systems are highly temperamental.

Featuring a heavy armament, and protected by a shell of interlocking armor plates, the Desdinova is built to flank the enemy while ignoring fast threats. An array of Arcjet engines propel the ship's bulk during its bone-rattling combat maneuvers. Primarily known for catching and annihilating frigates and destroyers, it's also capable of avoiding most ships it cannot duel. While it consumes more hyperspace fuel than any other destroyer - not to mention the maintenance required to keep it running - the Desdinova can usually tear apart enough targets to make up for its flaws.

A useful ship in a small fleet, the Weevil is one of the most popular Blackrock exports. Its large size allows it to incorporate a drone bay and a decent fuel and cargo capacity, but its specs leave something to be desired when compared to a true combat frigate.

This class started its life as a pre-Collapse asteroid smasher, and a hundred years of design iteration eventually resulted in a decent military vessel. Rugged, flexible, and hard to destroy, it's an economical choice for direct combat. Every Gonodactylus carries a repurposed Mark IV Asteroid Impactor, which can be used as a one-shot surprise attack in battle.

"I lost mine, got ambushed by a phase group in the Rama system. I drifted back into a patrol clamped to half a turret module. Heatsinks handling my suit's load through contact... in an induced coma, should've died in the blowout, but here I am. Then, this gunner I met told me about how he got himself wrecked out in a belt somewhere. He'd opened the main console, poked around for a day and found old survey programs that worked with the main sensors. Found himself a hollow asteroid, some hideout - it kept him alive until they saw the beacon. You get my point? These things are one-of-a-kind. They want to keep us in them because they work, and they last, and you try to find me a shield that won't throw sparks trying to stop that ridiculous Mark Four..."

A monstrous multirole fighter that rapidly chews through hull and armor if given the chance. Its size makes it vulnerable to some anti-ship weapons, but its thick armor is an asset against many anti-fighter weapons.

While it can be a big target compared to other fighters, the Krait is almost untouchable when facing enemy interceptors and light frigates. Any unshielded target has to fear a strafing run from the Krait's twin INMs and rotary cannon, but because it lacks a shield, it can get caught in crossfire. Commanders are advised to deploy them to the right zones of battle, so that their strengths can outweigh their weaknesses.

A nasty little skirmisher with few glaring weaknesses, save for a lack of missile weapons. With lots of wide-traversal hybrid turrets and a trump card system that often gets its crew out of trouble, the Goblin is the iconic Blackrock picket ship, and excels at competently destroying light targets such as fighters, frigates, and undergunned brigands.

The Mantis can unleash a storm of missiles or torpedoes, and has unrivalled mobility. These traits make it perfect for wolfpacks comprised of various attack frigates, or for lurking around the edges of a battle, ready to launch its payload.

Designed around a formidable drive system, the Mantis is designed to attack much larger ships than itself by virtue of outmaneuvering its escorts or being able to charge in if an opening presents itself. However, thanks to its high specs, it's able to comfortably duel most other frigates, although this is often considered a wasteful use of its firepower.

Cramped and uncomfortable, the ship's innards resemble those of a much more primitive vessel, as most of the space inside the hull is taken up by conduits, ammo wells, thruster sinks and capacitors. Those crews who actually take a liking to the Mantis tend to be hotheads who revel in their spartan condition.

Carries a high-yield, unguided plasma torpedo. The Vespa's raw firepower can bring down the biggest targets, but its payload is unreliable against faster, smaller ships.

The Vespa is designed around speed and firepower, like most Blackrock ships. Provided the bombers don't get shot down, their engine power allows them to rearm quickly.

A small turreted particle beam helps the Vespa intercept enemy artillery fire en-route, and can help it survive against weak interceptors or enemy SRMs. The bomber is still defenseless against enemy frigates or fighters, so choosing the right attack vector is important. Large fleets of Vespas can be hard to assault for enemy fighters, as the precision of the particle beams adds up to form a lethal defensive network.

Designed to counter modern battlespace tactics, the Kurmaraja dominates in a fight defined by fast threats and fighter projection. Fast, carrier-capable and heavily armed, its tactical freedom is nearly unmatched among capital ships.

Blackrock's main capital ship for many cycles, this is a subtle ship compared to the brutal Karkinos. The physics-defying plating that covers the Kurmaraja is the key to its strength. Interdimensional forcefields clamp down on the drive anchors of enemy ships, while pinpoint scalar field excitations distort space around projectiles, stopping them from reaching their targets. Caught dead in the water, smaller ships are easily destroyed, and larger ships may find their firepower ineffective.

Crewmembers tend to complain about its tangled grid layout and mazelike interior: the Kurmaraja sacrifices comfort and convenience for its ability to field fighters and power its various systems at the same time. When prepped for combat, much of the ship is sealed off in order to protect the crew from emissions, which empties out most of the two side decks and leads to the infamous ''Kurma Crunch'' in the core, battlestations and bridge.

Armed with Quill launchers that are effective against both shields and light armor, the Serket is capable of applying good pressure from medium distances, aided by its powerful engines and durable frame.

The Cetonia-class Freighter is a tough nut to crack, and can even serve as a reliable, but uncomfortable personnel transport. Comes with some built-in defensive weapons and a front shield for use against "navigational hazards".

Ultimately, the Cetonia represents a good compromise between efficiency, tonnage and emergency performance, and has proven its worth to many traders and corporate agents. Some hulls have famously been upgraded and militarized to the point where they can contend with attack frigates, but Corporate doctrine frowns on this practice, recommending spacers use tried-and-tested measures such as investing in other Corporate defense products, like the Goblin-class Gunship or Robberfly-class Corvette...

Built long ago as a self-sufficient exploration and research vessel, the once-obscure Stenos has recently been produced in greater numbers by the Blackrock Corporation as a mobile base for tech-mining and other long-distance operations. In direct combat, the Stenos can be quite effective when backed up by escorts, especially when fighting off weaker pirates and other opportunists, thanks to its wide weapon coverage and powerful shield emitter.

The class is named after Hannibal Stenos, the scientist and pioneer who laid the foundations for the corporation's transformation into a Sector power.
The Typheus-class Light Carrier is fast and reasonably self-sufficient, allowing it to adjust its position relative to the fleet to best bring its fighter complement and support weapons to bear.

One of the few corvette-sized ships that still find widespread usage, often seen in the role of a scoutship, harasser or courier. Lacks a shield generator, but its sheer speed makes it a slippery target.

A piece of Blackrock tech that has made its way into the Sector at large, the Robberfly is about as small and light as a combat-rated ship can realistically be. Its greatest strength is exceptional agility, granted by a full-body impulse engine with remarkable output for its size. Because much of the hull is occupied by sensory equipment, weapon mounts, and engine nozzles, overall hull strength is left wanting, and there is no room for a shield emitter. Still, the Robberfly has a reputation for security in civilian settings due to its low sensor footprint and its ability to simply evade interception. When pressed to fight, however, a Robberfly captain must use the ship's speed for all it's worth.

Robberflies can be assembled in a matter of hours from modular components given proper facilities, so they can be found practically anywhere spacers get into trouble, largely due to Blackrock's major reform of their Interstellar Exports Policy back in '178.

A fully-fledged battleship designed for a post-Domain reality, the Karkinos is Blackrock's final stake in the struggle for Sector dominance. Its firepower and ability to relocate gives it unmatched first-strike capability.

The Karkinos allows one to directly observe the raw power required to bridge distances in realspace. Like some of the Domain's last designs, this battleship can perform tactical teleports - but instead of neatly shifting through spacetime, the Karkinos forces itself through a temporary rift in reality, a chaotic process that makes it impossible for an outside observer to predict the ship's destination. While inefficient compared to a phase teleporter, the unwieldy waste energy created by the Scalar Deracinator is a weapon unto itself.

Due to emissions from the ship's core, it's visible even where other ships would be mistaken for a piece of debris - so the Karkinos is only seen in large, well-defended fleets that don't need to hide from enemy sensors. 

The corona that surrounds the Dimensional Engine is bleedthrough from extradimensional space: it forms a permanent link, analogous to a massive drive anchor. The construction of a Karkinos begins in earnest when this link has been created; the rest of the ship then has to be built around it. According to official reports, not all projects lead to spaceworthy hulls. Many get scrapped after core construction is complete because they do not meet safety specifications - but in a single, widely-reported worst case, construction ended in a disaster involving the destruction and disappearance of an entire dry dock.

The Convergence is an ungainly but spacious vessel that exerts a deceptive amount of control over the battlespace through its fighter bays and missile weapons. Its cargo capacity, fuel reserves, and ease of maintenance have kept the Convergence in active service; across many campaigns, it has a habit of paying for itself.

A fearsome vessel, the Asura has access to completely overwhelming firepower, but can quickly reach its limits in the hands of a careless captain. Due to its curious development history, the ship is considered a rare sight in the Sector today.

To even the odds in an increasingly likely all-out war between Blackrock and an entrenched Sector power, the Development Board once had several teams competing against each other to create a mobile anti-capital cruiser with siege capabilities. Unfortunately, the Asura Project came just short of reaching important milestones, and priorities shifted shortly before the Sovereignty Conflict began in earnest.

Losing out to the Adv. Cruiser Upgrade Program, the Asura Project should have been cancelled - however, the upcoming war meant the Corporation desperately needed a heavy-hitting cruiser that would not tax the primary production lines. The team was tasked with finding a way to make their half-finished design into a viable warship.

The Asura blueprint underwent an endless string of last-minute alterations. The Dimensional Engine originally intended for the main gun had to be replaced with a capital-grade standard power core. A system was installed in order to keep its output in check unless the mission demanded maximum performance. The skeletal Asura was then rigged out with engines, turret wells and armor components from older, tried-and-tested designs, resulting in an expensive, but surprisingly well-integrated and effective warship.

Embodying the current peak of Blackrock technology, the Imaginos is capable of reversing and nullifying enemy firepower, constantly shifting around the battlespace, and regenerating its own armor. This comes at the cost of being unable to power a shield generator.

The Imaginos' effectiveness as a combat ship has been heavily debated. Corporate doctrine demands deploying the ship into combat in order to gain valuable data and to fuel further development - at least, that was the initial rationale behind risking such valuable assets. Nowadays, the Imaginos is pressed into service in order to narrowly tip the scales in large battles and guarantee the Corporation's survival, where elite pilots can wield its abilities to nullify massive carrier strikes, interdict key targets, and upend enemy strategies.

This cheap but effective unmanned interceptor becomes deadlier in swarms, but its weapons do little against larger targets. Extreme agility lets them excel at dogfighting and missile interception, and their Shredders are great against damaged ships.

Little more than a pair of enormous engines and a Shredder with a tiny support module sandwiched in between, the Dipteron can be pushed harder than any other interceptor, especially thanks to its complete automation, allowing acceleration and changes of direction that would pulp any human pilot.

These enormous, fortified ships form the backbone of the Blackrock Drive Yards industrial machine. Within its armored shell, the Eschaton can protect a great haul of cargo, fuel and personnel, and various military-grade systems enable it to join an engagement alongside its escorts. Nonetheless, it is not as economically efficient as competing logistics ships in any given role, as the design prioritizes security and flexibility.

Many of Blackrock Drive Yards' original hulls are answers to industrial sabotage from competitors, rebels, and political enemies. One example of this is the Eschaton, which had to be devised as a result of the Tri-Tachyon Corporation's efforts to deny access to Atlas blueprints. Few had foreseen that Blackrock would be able to muster the industrial strength to prototype many of their ambitious projects, much less mass-produce and refine them over time.

An excellent addition to any fleet that expects to see combat, the Scorpion has long served as Blackrock's main ship of the line, playing the important role of fire support and anvil to the hammer of strike frigates and fighters.

As the Corporation's influence and power grew, so did the desire to make themselves independent of Domain autofacs and the political concerns that came with them. The Scorpion is modelled on the Enforcer and Hammerhead-class destroyers, eschewing combat speed to focus on staying power. While it can't dodge enemy missiles and torpedoes, the Scorpion comes with its own escorts, the Stinger-class drones. They provide cover fire and a convenient barrier, even as the mothership vents flux or becomes incapacitated, making the Scorpion one of Blackrock's most well-defended warships.

The Silverfish is a classic high-end frigate, often used as the personal shuttle of corporate agents, or as a secure small-scale freighter. Its impressive speed makes it hard to intercept.

The Silverfish is a 'light capital-class' frigate: a small, self-sufficient vessel that has an answer to most threats presented by similar ships, which can support itself through its cargo capacity and easily maintained systems. The Silverfish eschews firepower in favor of a well-armored hull, and packs a reliable powerplant and shield generator. Its three converging turrets provide a decent battery, but a Silverfish armed for ship-to-ship combat doesn't have room for point defense.
This ship is rarely spotted outside of corporate fleets. Compared to the regular issue Silverfish, it's tougher and more fit for combat, but retains good cargo capacity and flexibility.

This Silverfish has been altered or built according to a widely circulated upgrade package, turning it into an armored gunboat. Retaining the base hull's excellent speed, the Mod Silverfish can tear into enemy flanks and duel combat frigates. Popular among well-connected privateers and pirates.

''We are one of the few yards willing to touch Blackrock systems directly. The details stay off the manifest, and the work is routed through intermediaries for your protection and ours.

Your Mod Silverfish Mark II package adds scaffolded side weapon sponsons, rebuilt autoloaders, and reinforced cargo compartments intended to pass long-range scans. The guns are vintage; the feed systems are new. Side turrets are restricted to cannons because the autoloaders were built around compact ballistic feed paths.
 
Safeguards kept grid performance above the minimum export threshold, and the epidermal armor has been expanded to fit the extra hardware. Some of our customers remain within the Corporation itself, so ownership is not automatically incriminating.

If you have further questions, keep this console active. May your Mod Silverfish be your bridge to the stars.

- T. Handling, Handling Shipworks''

This early production model of the Nevermore lacks a Dimensional Engine, but has powerful thrusters and better armor protection. The 0-series deviates too much from the current Nevermore to undergo modernization, so the few combat-ready hulls in the Sector are used as ceremonial ships or piloted by select individuals through circumstance. 

While outdated, this ship is not to be underestimated - especially when it comes to long-range combat; its modular armament is actually heavier than that of its successors.
Grants a burst of speed and maneuverability, allowing the ship to quickly turn or move in a given direction. Holds 2 charges that replenish quickly.
Activates powerful auxiliary engines that propel the ship forward at great speed. Allows for slight turning and disables shields while active.
The Sentinel Drone is a flexible escort drone that provides suppression fire, point defense and a convenient distraction. A reserve drone is prepared before combat.
Causes a subjective 6x time dilation effect, allowing higher mobility and firepower for a short duration. Disables shields while active.
Tears a hole into spacetime: the resulting void sucks the ship through to its destination, carrying with it a dangerous bow-shock of phase plasma and multidimensional energy filaments.

Slows enemy ships and projectiles in a wide radius. Some projectiles are deflected away from the ship. Self-propelled missiles can counteract the braking effect, and ships with greater mass are more resistant as well. Finally, the system builds hard flux while active and disables shields.
Rows of powerful, vectored thrusters grant a burst of speed and maneuverability. Holds 3 charges that replenish over time.
Deploys four advanced fighter drones that can protect the mothership or attack a target at medium range.
Deploys a single heavy defensive drone armed with point defense weapons, anti-fighter missiles and a Particle Lance.
Tears a hole into spacetime: the resulting void sucks the ship through to its destination, carrying with it a dangerous bow-shock of phase plasma and multidimensional energy filaments.
This drone bay can maintain, field and communicate with two Sentinel drones simultaneously.

Allows the ship's reactor to reach full output for a short duration. Boosts rate of fire and lowers flux cost for energy weapons, and gives a minor boost to speed and mobility. After the system deactivates, spent flux sinks are ejected, granting a 3x boost to flux dissipation for 1 second.
Resets the cooldown of all weapons, allowing them to be fired again immediately. Builds flux when used.
These miniature burst jets grant a brief speed boost to any fighter-sized craft.
Launches two self-propelled cannons. Designed for suppressive HE fire, they cannot stray far from the mothership, and have a front shield for defense.

A geologically unstable planet due to the constant tidal pull of Lodestone, Blackrock is a ball of molten rock covered in a thick atmosphere. An orbital mirror is part of a long-abandoned terraforming project, but the surface is dotted with industrial complexes, and there are several space elevators with spacedocks. Fragments of a detached orbital ring are now attached to what remains of the pre-collapse equatorial space elevator complex.

A cosmopolitan world whose citizens enjoy a rather high standard of living. A dense mantle stabilizes the crust, but makes for a weak magnetic field. The crustacean-like lifeforms native to Lodestone are extremely resistant to radiation, and barely evolve. Human settlements are widespread around the poles, where the climate is relatively stable.
text3: The cantina you're in offers a breathtaking view of the Blackrock-Lodestone system. Lining the walls are various plaques showing off antique drawings of exotic lifeforms from Lodestone. In rows underneath are more artistic interpretations of hideous space monsters that seem like twisted versions of the former creatures.

text1: Verge is a gas giant whose entire atmosphere is infested by a rampant, competitive ecosystem of nanite pseudolife. Its orbit is clogged with the debris of warships, coffin vessels from the Exodus, and hibernating pseudo-organisms. Security sentries surround the planet and eradicate anything trying to exit or enter low orbit. Approach is strongly discouraged.
text3: Trying to approach Verge, your fleet is instantly pinged by transmission bursts from more than 12 different sentries and nanosats. You decide against investigating closer.

text1: Gneiss is a yellow main sequence star that slowly nears the end of its life cycle, mundane except for its unstable stellar weather pattern.

This massive space fortress is tethered to the Port Augustmoon space elevator complex on Lodestone. A regional center of shipbreaking, commerce and industry, it also offers a breathtaking view of the Blackrock-Lodestone system and its orbital mirror.

Lydia is a small world, devoid of natural resources or strategical value. Owned and operated by a small cabal of ex-BRDY associates for private purposes, the planet has an open port for trade of any sort, especially discrete less-than-legal deals.

Preclusion lives up to its namesake; most of its population descends from exiles, criminals, and rejected citizens of numerous factions. Notably, this planet's regime is overseen by an illusive pirate lord who has managed to levy enough political power to prevent the Blackrock military from taking over.

A massive orbital installation stemming from an ancient terraforming project, since upscaled and maintained by BRDY. By deflecting sunlight bound for Blackrock, it keeps the planet barely habitable. Its likeness was used for the corporate logo.

By name, Blackrock Drive Yards is megacorporation that offers propulsive solutions, arms tech and advanced medical equipment. As their only leverage is in the carefully controlled trade of unique technologies, and because their keys to power are all tied to the same system, they have been forced to build up and maintain a massive war machine. Blackrock has engaged in both open and clandestine conflict against many extant and past Sector powers, increasingly turning into a withdrawn, authoritarian corpo-state centered on its namesake planet.

Blackrock rarely works through proxies - any loyal spacer is a Blackrock Associate, free to pursue their own goals as long as it does not conflict with the corporation's interests. However, even the most enterprising contractors find that their ambitions terminate below a certain level; the inner circle of the faction is impenetrable, and rife with dubious rumors and unseen pitfalls.

A geologically unstable planet due to the constant tidal pull of Lodestone, Blackrock is a barren world covered in a thick atmosphere. The birthplace of the Blackrock Drive Yards corporation, it's one of the most industrialized planets in the Sector.
text3:
The uninhabitable surface bakes in sweltering heat, barely mitigated by basic terraforming measures. Even so, the planet supports a sprawling urban society, distributed among eight massive arcologies, some of which burrow into the crust, and others which stretch into the upper cloud layers. The rest of Blackrock is sectioned by massive automated industrial complexes and subterranean research facilities.

Taking in the view, you can't seem to tell how far those structures in the distance reach into the atmosphere, or how far from the surface you're currently located. Now and then, a light pierces the haze outside the window and forces you to squint, strobing as it slowly fades away.

text1: Oceans of dust and sand cover this world, and among them, bioengineered crops thrive in the red sunlight. After Blackrock Drive Yards annexed Bharata from independent rule, the planet briefly became the center of a renewed colonization effort, with settlers being offered grants in an attempt to increase the agricultural value of the planet.
text3:
When Bharata later became the site of Blackrock Drive Yards' new forward base of operations, the immigration rates once again increased, and the planet has become a popular target for a certain kind of ambitious individualist. But despite its growth, Bharata is no paradise.

Port Sentinel is the surface spacedock on Bharata. One of the more urbanized spots on the planet, it's usually where passing spacers end up. Looking around the area, you see jumpsuited Blackrock privateers going in and out of ready rooms, and groups of dead-eyed traders making their way towards different terminals.

text1:
A lifeless rock, Staalo's strong magnetic field somewhat mitigates the radiation it recieves from orbiting through the bow shock of Rama and Sita's stellar winds. Rich with rare minerals, the planet has several large mining complexes.

Once the capital of industry in the Rama system, Staalo has been depopulated as a consequence of the system's history of war. Now gradually being reduced to a staging base for the Hegemony forces in the system, Staalo still serves as the home of a considerable population of workers, permanent residents and loyalists. Some stay put due to grudges with Blackrock or deep ties to the Hegemony government, while others would do anything to escape their current situation, but lack the means to do so.
text3:
The dark and barren surface of Staalo belies the planet's long history of human influence. The sprawling settlement beneath the main spaceport houses 90% of the planet's population at any given time. A brief elevator ride lands you in a massive hollowed-out hab nexus, proof of people's efforts to stake out a living on this hostile world.

In the visitor hub, Hegemony propaganda slandering the Blackrock Drive Yards corporation blares from a ceiling-mounted vidscreen, while a pair of security officers escort off some furious-looking workers clad in the signature dust jackets of the Staaloan working class. In spite of the everpresent patrols, the Hegemony garrison seems to have long turned jaded towards the graffiti and the soapboxing, wide-eyed street preachers.

text1: Thoroughly damaged during the past cycles of war, this orbital has been claimed and rebuilt by Tri-Tachyon for the Corporation's own purposes. The station officially serves as the base of a long-term research program, that aims to discover what happened to the planets that became Rama's outer and inner debris rings.
text3: Now mostly a transfer point for combat fleets heading into the Rama system, Thalm Listening Post is a popular place for daring travellers to rid themselves of 'interesting' cargo while catching up on the latest information about the Gneiss Expanse. Tri-Tachyon military patrols and PR officials are present so that they may 'helpfully' guide those who seem out-of-place, confused or nervous into small side corridors for 'guidance', and most of the people in the area are mechanically heading to or fro the trade hub or one of several docking terminals.

text1: A frozen world bombarded by sleet from Rama's vast debris rings, Limbo's complete hostility to human settlement and surface-to-space travel has paradoxically resulted in the birth of a small settlement that serves as the ideal haven for those who seek to evade detection from the Sector at large.
A gas giant mostly notable for its abnormal magnetic field, Senroamin shields the inner system from sleet and comets and allows Bharata to remain habitable.

Designed for use in dangerous and unexplored territory, this sleek, high-performance vessel can fend for itself and carry its own supplies and fuel. Branded as an exploration and scouting vessel by Blackrock, the Megaceras is otherwise decried as having been deliberately created for sting operations and surveillance outside of Blackrock's home space.

Featuring a triplet high-power arc jet engine married to a drive core that consumes most of the reactor's power output, it can beat many destroyers in terms of speed and burn, but its armament of light weapons won't stand up to big warships.

Fires slow-moving energy orbs that home in on enemy targets. An unpredictable flight path and a lack of physical components makes the orbs hard to shoot down.

A highly advanced artillery weapon, the Scalaron Pulse is classified as a missile due to its tactical role and the fact that it homes in on enemy targets, but the similarities end there.

To launch a pulse, dimensionally entangled plasma is pumped into a bubble of nanites that acts as an antenna, guidance computer and containment field guide. By slowly bleeding plasma in a given direction, the pulse's trajectory can be altered in order to home in on targets, but it's an imperfect process that results in a jagged, unpredictable path. Since the pulse itself is all payload, yield when striking a target is very high, and the containment field's breach causes an arcing discharge that can wreak havoc on a ship's weapons and engines.

Projects a sustained beam of raw energy that grows in power based on your flux level. Exceeding max. flux while firing the beam will trigger an automatic emergency shutdown, disabling the weapon temporarily.

Almost nothing is known about this weapon's functioning, and especially, nothing is known about the detailed workings of its myriad failsafe mechanisms. Curiously, thanks to various incidents throughout the Collapse, Exodus and later cataclysmic events, its design name has been lost to time, and several monickers have been thrown around by those who worked on Project Morpheus.

Fires a beam of energy guided by the Morpheus' field projectors, that curves to home in on enemy targets. Beams rapidly fizzle out if they fail to hit their target.

Deep pre-Collapse breakthroughs in dimensional warping technology gave rise to a number of incomparably complicated weapon designs, and in the modern era, most of them fail to function or are actively dangerous to fire, if built into modern single-core powerplant testbeds. Blackrock researchers came to discover that the Morpheus' tri-core powerplant, with its finicky tuning parameters and inherent instability, was designed in tandem with a few of these arcane weapons.

Thanks to poorly understood accumulate effects of the three competing rifts, a large forcefield can be extended into shallow subspace, and temporary weaknesses can be created and shifted around freely. This process is controlled by an embedded Savant/Pre-Alpha level AI (as opposed to the more conventional Operating Mind) that exclusively observes an umwelt-space of relevant data, and is slaved to the field modulators.

As a failsafe, it seems that the core cognition unit of the Morpheus re-initializes this AI whenever a salvo is fired, and then immediately culls and resets it until the next time it needs to "wake up", preventing it from reaching an unpredictable internal state.

The LFO, or Light Filament Ordnance, is the final product of all these interacting systems - a homing weapon that can reliably guide a trapped scalaron ray into a chosen target, with strike and kill rates that exceed most missile weapons, and infinite reloads provided by the ship's Dimensional Engine. Modularizing this technology would be extremely useful - but its nature seems to forbid it.

An extremely powerful prototype ship that treats the laws of physics as a vague guideline, and costs as much as a capital ship to maintain and manufacture.

While little is known about Project Morpheus, its existence is not a secret, given the confirmed sightings of spaceworthy offshoot hulls - although open inquiry is a surefire way to attract ''interest'' from Blackrock, Tri-Tachyon and other parties.
A combination of weak dimensional lift and conventional thrusters grants the ship extreme agility and improved speed for a short duration. Cannot reverse. Holds 3 charges that replenish over time.

This planet has been profoundly altered by an event in the Sector's past - likely to have coincided with the destruction of many of the other planets in the Rama system. No permanent orbitals exist.

The inner composition of Vena remains a mystery. Not even remote-controlled probes can survive for long on the planet's surface.
text3:
Legions of specialized drones coast the bow shock of Vena's magnetosphere, bringing data back to their owners and sabotaging each other with constant electronic warfare. Occasionally, fights are known to break out between Tri-Tachyon and Blackrock task forces in high orbit.

As you approach the outer energy filaments, a mess of warnings clutter up your displays and scopes, followed by a barrage of ominous comm bursts from TT and BRDY nanosats.

text1: A large-caliber offshoot of the Gale Cannon, developed as a main gun for the prototype Nevermore ACL. Fires punishing, charged anti-warship rounds.

Armored, fast and reliable, this combat destroyer is usually seen paired with the slower Scorpion. Its role is often defined by the weapon slotted into the large hardpoint.

Designed to relieve the ageing stock of corporate Desdinova hulls, the Eurypterus is a fast destroyer that can give chase to frigates and avoid big threats on the battlefield. Recognizing the need for utility in attrition battles, the Eurypterus was also made to act as a mobile gun platform, thanks to its modular heavy hardpoint. Its maintenance budget is fairly low, due to extended use of standardized components: an attempt to reduce the corporation's reliance on long supply lines.

A low-flux weapon that fires a pulsed particle beam, disrupting enemy shields and electronics. Highly effective against light targets, especially when fired in volleys.

The Particle Lance fires a stream of mixed particles through a tunnel of faint interdimensional weakness created by a tracer high-energy scalaron. It causes a wide range of disruptive effects on impact, and is remarkably flux-efficient. Lack of thermal energy makes it inefficient against armor, just like the related Argus family of weapons. Even so, synchronized strikes against light armor will eventually ablate enough mass to deal heavy damage.
This massive space station is used for research and high-orbit monitoring of Verge, and also serves as a listening outpost for the outer Gneiss system.
text3:
Vigil Station is home to a diverse population of researchers, new Blackrock associates looking to prove themselves, refugees from the Expanse, and exiles from the inner Gneiss system. It also serves as a shipbreaking center, relieving the space elevator on Lodestone. 

Most habitable areas are overseen by everpresent Blackrock Internal Affairs officers and sentry bots, especially the intermediate areas leading to the Corporate sections. The top of the station houses a colony of refugees, opportunists and dissidents, a giant ring-shaped shanty town of people that lack the funds or connections to leave or move up in the station's hierarchy.

text1:
Pulverizes light targets with a barrage of particle bolts. Long range and flux-efficient, making it useful as a support weapon.

Derived from the same technology used in the Argus Particle Beam, the Particle Rifle uses a grooved, open magnetic ''sling'' to accelerate charged particles. The pulsed ray is less effective against armor and shields, but rapidly destroys unprotected ships, and destroys missiles and rockets in a crossfire.
A reliable launch platform for the fast, volatile T-1 Fury plasma torpedo. A built-in capacitor removes the rack/single mount's flux cost, but increases cooldown time.

A direct-fire, weakly guided anti-armor missile. Easily defeats point defense due to raw thrust, but can miss small, agile targets. Generous ammo reserves for its size.

One of Blackrock's oldest space engine designs eventually found new life in the Rage SRM. The missile gains extreme speed moments after launch, easily propelling its shaped-charge warhead past point defenses. The weapon's homing ability leaves something to be desired, so care should be taken to aim the launcher at the target before firing.

Fires slow-moving energy orbs that home in on enemy targets. An unpredictable flight path and a lack of physical components makes the orbs hard to shoot down. Bigger ammo reserves and more punch than the small-mount version.

A highly advanced artillery weapon, the Scalaron Pulse is classified as a missile due to its tactical role and the fact that it homes in on enemy targets, but the similarities end there.

To launch a pulse, dimensionally entangled plasma is pumped into a bubble of nanites that acts as an antenna, guidance computer and containment field guide. By slowly bleeding plasma in a given direction, the pulse's trajectory can be altered in order to home in on targets, but it's an imperfect process that results in a jagged, unpredictable path. Since the pulse itself is all payload, yield when striking a target is very high, and the containment field's breach causes an arcing discharge that can wreak havoc on a ship's weapons and engines.

A pod of direct-fire, weakly guided anti-armor missiles. Easily defeats point defense due to raw thrust, but can miss small, agile targets.

One of Blackrock's oldest space engine designs eventually found new life in the Rage SRM. Because the missile's thruster is, essentially, a miniaturized orbital burn drive, it gains extreme speed moments after launch, easily propelling its shaped-charge warhead past point defenses. The weapon's homing ability leaves something to be desired, so care should be taken to aim the launcher at the target before firing.

Fires a dense, spinning disc that imparts a massive amount of kinetic energy on impact. More effective against large targets due to slow projectiles, the Bolide can provide devastating short-term damage and will crush its way through compromised armor sections.

A massive electromagnetic acceleration cannon, the Bolide's internal space is occupied by flywheels that spin up disc-shaped projectiles to store great amounts of kinetic energy. The Bolide is devastating against shields, and its projectiles tend to fragment violently inside spaced armor, granting it extra kill power.

Although the Bolide is easiest to use against heavy warships, the ultra-dense discs fired by this weapon can be given extra speed by quickly accelerating in a given direction before firing. When mounted on fast, Burst Jet-equipped ships, skilled captains can "sling" discs at their targets, making up for the weapon's biggest flaw. Heavier ships that can't accelerate quickly tend to rely on the Bolide's large effective range and shield-crushing strength.

Fires dense, spinning discs that impart a heavy amount of kinetic energy on impact. Low damage over time, but impressive short-term burst damage, that can quickly overload an unwary target or defeat weak armor.

A rugged hybrid warship that has seen heavy use throughout the Blackrock Corporation's history, the Knight has a great missile carrying capacity that synergizes well with its fighter bays and powerful drive systems.

Fires a barrage of slow, proximity-fuzed plasma charges. Great against missiles and fighters, but greater flux buildup than most PD weapons.

The ''Defender'' Plasma Charge Cannon, commonly known as the Burst Cannon, is one of many blueprints acquired by Blackrock and since repurposed. The cannon is notable for its large turret assembly compared to most light weapons, where the gyros and actuators are mounted inside the turret house. The internals are instead occupied by cooling systems. A simple electrochemical firing mechanism is used to mitigate the considerable flux buildup of charging the shells, which leads to a fairly low muzzle velocity.

Its psychological effect on fighter pilots is well-documented inside the Corporation.
Boosts the ship's speed and mobility for a short duration. Bigger boost and less duration compared to standard Maneuvering Jets.

A large and fast interceptor, designed to afterburn in and tackle strike craft and other light threats. A Taipan wing can easily cripple a frigate, but is bereft of anti-armor firepower and needs support when attacking well-defended targets.
A light bomber that offers strike capability without burdening the carrier's hangars too much. Its excellent speed lets it keep up with other wings, but it's defenseless against fighters.

A support fighter designed to hover around friendly ships and protect them from enemy fighters and missiles. The Nadir is armed with potent point defense and suppression weapons, and protected by a durable front shield.

Barracuda drones are fast, expendable guard craft used by Blackrock's older drone-control suites. Their armament is simple, but four of them working together can screen missiles, harry fighters, and make a carrier's blind spots expensive to exploit.

Stinger drones are self-propelled gun mounts built around compact Gale Cannon hardware. They do not wander far from their mothership, but inside their control radius they provide the kind of close fire support that makes an exposed armor plate feel like a mistake.

Sentinel drones are flexible escort platforms, equal parts suppression weapon, point-defense screen, and disposable distraction. Blackrock crews like them because they buy seconds; Blackrock accountants like them because losing a drone is still cheaper than losing the ship it was covering.

Bulwark drones are heavy defensive escorts intended to make the space around a Blackrock hull unpleasant for fighters, torpedoes, and impatient captains. Their point-defense package and Particle Lance give them enough bite to be more than passive armor with thrusters.
Launches flares that attract enemy PD fire.
Most of the Taipan's speed comes from this powerful afterburner.

Integral to the Stenos' design, these drones perform a wide range of functions. In combat, they serve as a convenient distraction, using their utility plasma cutters to chew through enemy targets. Slow and somewhat vulnerable, but lethal against unshielded ships.

Overwhelms shields and light armor with a twin-barrel barrage of large-caliber Shards. Special ammo increases lethality against armor and hull compared to inert kinetic rounds.

The culmination of many cycles of research, the aptly named Mega Shard Cannon maximizes the overall rate of fire that is possible for Phase Shard munitions with a peculiar recoil-compensating armature and hybrid chemical/coil-propelled firing mechanism. Ammo for this weapon is particularly crude compared to that of its smaller counterparts, as the larger caliber ensures lethality either way, and ammo availability and ease of manufacture is at a premium.

A combat-rated tanker, designed for uninterrupted operation of supply lines in the face of encroaching hostility, privateering and enemy strike ops. The Hawkmoth is notorious for being difficult prey, thanks to its monstrous thrusters, dense armor and respectable armament. In fact, the Hawkmoth tends to simply get deployed into battle as though it was a regular warship.

Its dual-purpose design makes the 'Moth a great investment for a commander that expects to see frequent contact with hostile ships, but its raw efficiency at transporting fuel is not as great as other tankers. All things considered, the Corporation is not shy of selling surplus hulls to outsiders, as most end up being used for scavenging or contract work aligned with their interests.

A ship based on the Blackrock Weevil, sacrificing its drone bay and most of its cargo holds for greater overall power and a wider selection of small ordnance. A comparison with the Mod Silverfish points to a common originator, especially since the Zabrus is often called the ''Mod Weevil''. A large number of hulls found their way into the wrong hands several cycles ago, which has contributed to the ship's infamy.

The Antaeus integrates elements from several competing schools of warship design, thanks to incremental upgrades and alterations to what began as a Mastery-epoch heavy cruiser platform. It is a strong fleet anchor that serves an important (and completely unique) role in Blackrock fleet doctrine.

Absolutely vital to all large-scale Corporate military operations, the Antaeus has been mass-produced for countless cycles. In addition to the current "factory model" Antaeus, several ancient hulls are also still in circulation, some even having been liberated from enemy hands and cycled back into service after undergoing a modernization refit.
A pulsed low-frequency laser, which features a dedicated secondary power supply that must be built into a ship's deep flux dispersion network. Very effective against missiles.
Very quickly warps the ship along its current vector. Long cooldown.

A decent, not-quite-ideal tool for most combat situations, the Straff can be deceptively deadly in the right hands.

Blackrock got their hands on this unpopular and somewhat obscure cannon blueprint during the chaos directly following the Collapse. The Development Board modernized it with a series of careful iterations, then monopolized the production of the Straff in order to make sure even the most run-down Corporate ships could mount a full complement of ship-to-ship weapons. In today's Sector, you hardly need to be well-connected to get your hands on a couple of these.

A heavy particle bolt weapon designed to fire rapid saturation bursts that are utterly devastating against unprotected ships, with long effective range to suppress smaller targets. If not blocked by shields, it will usually cause some significant temporary or permanent damage.

The Extreme Saturation Particle Assault Decoupled Array Repeater is the most optimized particle bolt weapon developed by Blackrock. Initially slated to replace the Particle Rifle, development involved so many power-hungry and ambitious tweaks that the ESPADA eventually required a large turret assembly for power feed and support mechanisms. This lends the weapon a few quirks; it is fairly easy to mount compared to other large energy weapons, and it has a good rotation and tracking speed thanks to its small size relative to the gyros and actuators.

The Dynastos excels at Sector-specific combat scenarios, such as defeating swarming attacks by outgunned, but aggressive light warships. A great asset for the discerning commander, the ship is self-sufficient, but can also serve as a competent heavy escort.

Being the product of a long-standing effort to optimize Dimensional Engine-carrying warships for mass production, Dynastos captains were initially hand-picked by the Consortium brass. Increasingly, the ship is being used to escort VIP fleets or to reinforce operations in the Sector rim, which means it has become available to a greater number of Blackrock-associated spacers.
Fires an energy pulse that attempts to home in on the target. Similar to the modular Scalaron Pulse Launcher, but faster, lower-yield and more unpredictable.

Fires a quick-moving bolt that explodes when something touches the containment field or once the field dissipates on its own, damaging everything in the area. Especially long range for an energy weapon, but highly flux-inefficient.
Causes a subjective 3x time dilation effect, combined with a massive boost to maximum speed. Disables shields while active, and builds some flux upon activation.
EMP burst attack, good against fighters that stray too close. Targets enemy missiles, ships and fighters up to 500 units away. Disables shields while active, but not weapons.
Delayed EMP attack with a 360-degree, 500 range radius. Produces scattered electric arcs followed by a final, powerful burst. During charge-up, builds flux and prevents your weapons from firing.

Designed to lead the charge against enemy formations, the Hellbender is encased in a thick shell of armor and carries flares to distract enemy point defenses. Armed with dual kinetic cannons and all-purpose plasma missiles.

A recklessly over-fed neutron particle cannon that can blow giant chunks out of any target. The Asura's reactor was designed with a special overdrive mode to enable sustained firing of this weapon - otherwise, careful trigger discipline is recommended.
Simple drones armed with triple rotary cannons and an ultralight missile rack. Good for tearing through missiles, fighters or damaged ships, but cannot stray far from the mothership.

A multi-stage missile that unleashes a blast of plasma bomblets. The Gridfire can be safely launched from backline ships to deliver a killing blow, and is especially hard to dodge due to its peculiar homing mechanism.
Charges up the ship's boosters for a short duration, preventing any form of acceleration or weapon firing, then grants a strong, committal burst of speed in the forward direction.

A multi-stage missile that unleashes a blast of plasma bomblets. The Gridfire can be safely launched from backline ships to deliver a killing blow, and is especially hard to dodge due to its peculiar homing mechanism.
This pod version has twice the volley size of the standard mount.

A single-barrel version of the Shredder. Enjoys marginally longer range, accuracy and muzzle velocity, but lacks bullet saturation, making it less reliable against missiles than the standard Shredder.

Strong for its size, the Dragonfly can deal great damage if left unchecked, and is an excellent force multiplier for frigate-based fleets.

Its Brakefield Barrier system can freeze and deflect incoming fire, letting the captain back out of a tough situation - in the simplest sense, it allows the Dragonfly to retreat once its reactor is close to redline, but there are some additional uses for this system. For instance, in a melee, the Dragonfly can prevent an enemy ship from moving, leaving it at the mercy of incoming heavy firepower. Fighters grappling with the Dragonfly can also be caught in this trap.

Projects a forcefield in a tight radius around the ship, that attempts to stop or deflect incoming projectiles and missiles. Fighters or small ships will also be affected if they are close enough. Prevents weapons from firing, but allows shields to remain active.
